gloss.en: to officially end a law or system

words.example: This tax should be abolished.

gloss.en: the deliberate ending of a pregnancy

words.example: Many people oppose abortion on moral grounds.

gloss.en: the state of being away from a place

words.example: The decision was made in my absence.

gloss.en: not present in a place

words.example: He was absent from work for two weeks.

gloss.en: extremely silly or not logical

words.example: That uniform makes the guards look absurd.

gloss.en: a large quantity that is more than enough

words.example: The park has an abundance of wildlife.

gloss.en: wrong or harmful use of something

words.example: Drug abuse is a serious problem.

gloss.en: a school or college for special training

words.example: She trained at the Royal Academy of Music.

gloss.en: to happen or make something happen faster

words.example: Inflation continues to accelerate.

gloss.en: the act of accepting an offer or invitation

words.example: Please confirm your acceptance of this offer.

gloss.en: that can be reached, entered or used

words.example: The remote area is accessible only by helicopter.

gloss.en: an impressive thing achieved after much work

words.example: It was one of his greatest accomplishments.

gloss.en: agreement with a rule or way of doing things

words.example: We acted in accordance with legal requirements.

gloss.en: in a way that suits the situation

words.example: We have to discover his plans and act accordingly.

gloss.en: being responsible and expected to explain actions

words.example: There are proposals for greater police accountability.

gloss.en: responsible and expected to explain your actions

words.example: The government should be held accountable.

gloss.en: to gradually get more and more of something

words.example: I seem to have accumulated a lot of books.

gloss.en: the process of gradually getting more of something

words.example: The accumulation of wealth took many years.

gloss.en: a statement that someone has done wrong

words.example: I won't make an accusation without proof.

gloss.en: a person on trial for committing a crime

words.example: The accused was found innocent.

gloss.en: a chemical with a pH below seven

words.example: Rye is tolerant of poor, acid soils.

gloss.en: the act of getting knowledge, a skill or something

words.example: She studies child language acquisition.

gloss.en: a unit for measuring an area of land

words.example: The estate has 3,000 acres of parkland.

gloss.en: the act of making something start working

words.example: The activation of these genes has two major effects.

gloss.en: a person who works for political or social change

words.example: She is a well-known human rights activist.

gloss.en: very serious or severe

words.example: There is an acute shortage of water.

gloss.en: the process of changing to suit a new situation

words.example: The adaptation of buildings for military use took time.

gloss.en: to stick to something

words.example: The bacteria adhere to the surface of the cells.

gloss.en: next to something

words.example: The planes landed on adjacent runways.

gloss.en: a small change made to correct or improve

words.example: I've made a few adjustments to the design.

gloss.en: to manage and organize an organization's affairs

words.example: She was chosen to administer the charity fund.

gloss.en: connected with organizing a business's work

words.example: It was just an administrative error.

gloss.en: a person who manages a company or institution

words.example: These decisions are made by the hospital administrators.

gloss.en: the act of letting someone into a place

words.example: Hospital admission is not necessary in most cases.

gloss.en: a young person developing from child to adult

words.example: This program helps adolescents between 13 and 18.

gloss.en: the act of legally taking another's child as your own

words.example: She put the baby up for adoption.

gloss.en: negative and unpleasant; unlikely to help

words.example: The flight was delayed by adverse weather conditions.

gloss.en: a person who supports a plan or cause

words.example: She is an advocate for hospital workers.

gloss.en: connected with beauty and art

words.example: I enjoy the aesthetic appeal of these songs.

gloss.en: the feeling of liking and caring about someone

words.example: Children need lots of love and affection.

gloss.en: the situation following an unpleasant event

words.example: Rebuilding took place in the aftermath of the war.

gloss.en: angry feelings leading to threats or violence

words.example: The research shows computer games may cause aggression.

gloss.en: connected with farming

words.example: The country changed its agricultural policy.

gloss.en: a person who helps an important official

words.example: White House aides briefed the press.

gloss.en: although

words.example: He finally agreed, albeit reluctantly, to help us.

gloss.en: quick to notice things; thinking clearly

words.example: Suddenly he found himself awake and fully alert.

gloss.en: strange and different from what you know

words.example: The city felt like an alien environment to her.

gloss.en: to put things in correct position, often in a line

words.example: Make sure the shelf is aligned with the cupboard.

gloss.en: arrangement of things in a straight line

words.example: The alignment of the sun and moon was rare.

gloss.en: very similar

words.example: My sister and I do not look alike.

gloss.en: an accusation made without giving proof

words.example: The police will investigate the allegation.

gloss.en: to state something as fact without proof

words.example: They allege that she was driving carelessly.

gloss.en: said to be true but without proof

words.example: These crimes were allegedly committed during the war.

gloss.en: an agreement between groups to work together

words.example: The two parties decided to form an alliance.

gloss.en: to officially give something for a purpose

words.example: A large sum was allocated for new library books.

gloss.en: an amount given to someone for a purpose

words.example: We have spent our entire allocation for the year.

gloss.en: money given regularly or for a purpose

words.example: He gets an allowance of $20 a day.

gloss.en: a country that agrees to help another

words.example: They asked their European allies for support.

gloss.en: a light, silver-grey metal

words.example: The pans are made of aluminium.

gloss.en: doing something for pleasure, not as a job

words.example: He is a keen amateur photographer.

gloss.en: a country's senior official in a foreign country

words.example: She is the British ambassador to Italy.

gloss.en: to change a law or document to improve it

words.example: He asked to see the amended version.

gloss.en: a small change to a document or law

words.example: They voted to propose an amendment to the law.

gloss.en: in the middle of or during something

words.example: He finished his speech amid tremendous applause.

gloss.en: a comparison between things with similar features

words.example: The teacher drew an analogy between the heart and a pump.

gloss.en: a heavy object dropped to hold a ship in place

words.example: The sailors decided to drop anchor in the bay.

gloss.en: a spirit believed to be a servant of God

words.example: The painting shows a host of angels.

gloss.en: with a name that is not known

words.example: The money came from an anonymous donor.

gloss.en: the equipment needed for a particular task

words.example: She picked up a piece of laboratory apparatus.

gloss.en: attractive or interesting

words.example: That idea wasn't a prospect I found appealing.

gloss.en: physical desire for food

words.example: He suffered from headaches and loss of appetite.

gloss.en: to show approval by clapping your hands

words.example: He started to applaud and the others joined in.

gloss.en: that can be said to be true in a case

words.example: Give details of children where applicable.

gloss.en: to choose someone for a job or position

words.example: They have appointed a new head teacher.

gloss.en: pleasure from recognizing good qualities

words.example: She shows little appreciation of good music.

gloss.en: not based on reason or plan; sometimes unfair

words.example: The choice of players seemed completely arbitrary.

gloss.en: relating to architecture and buildings

words.example: The building has many fine architectural features.

gloss.en: a collection of historical documents or records

words.example: The letters are kept in the national archive.

gloss.en: an area with seats around it for sports or shows

words.example: We went to a concert at the arena.

gloss.en: as can be reasonably argued or claimed

words.example: He is arguably the best actor of his generation.

gloss.en: a part of the body from shoulder to hand

words.example: He escaped with only a broken arm.

gloss.en: a large or impressive group of things

words.example: The shop had a vast array of bottles.

gloss.en: to express your thoughts clearly in words

words.example: She struggled to articulate her thoughts.

gloss.en: the powder left after something has burnt

words.example: He flicked cigarette ash onto the floor.

gloss.en: a strong desire to have or do something

words.example: I didn't realize you had political aspirations.

gloss.en: to strongly desire to achieve something

words.example: She aspired to a scientific career.

gloss.en: the murder of an important person

words.example: The president survived several assassination attempts.

gloss.en: the crime of physically attacking someone

words.example: Both men were charged with assault.

gloss.en: to gather or bring together as a group

words.example: All the students were asked to assemble in the hall.

gloss.en: a group elected to make decisions or laws

words.example: The new law was passed by the state assembly.

gloss.en: to state clearly that something is true

words.example: She continued to assert that she was innocent.

gloss.en: a statement that you strongly believe is true

words.example: He was correct in his assertion that they had lied.

gloss.en: a statement that something will certainly happen

words.example: They called for assurances about the policy.

gloss.en: protection a government gives to refugees

words.example: They fled the country to seek asylum.

gloss.en: a cruel and violent act, especially in war

words.example: In the war, both sides committed atrocities.

gloss.en: to succeed in getting something after effort

words.example: Most of our students attained five 'A' grades.

gloss.en: the act of being present at a place

words.example: Attendance at these lectures is not compulsory.

gloss.en: a lawyer, especially one acting in court

words.example: The prosecuting attorney made a short statement.

gloss.en: a quality or feature of someone or something

words.example: Patience is an important attribute in a teacher.

gloss.en: an official examination of financial records

words.example: The company carried out an annual audit.

gloss.en: real and not a copy

words.example: I don't know if the painting is authentic.

gloss.en: to give official permission for something

words.example: I can authorize payments up to £5,000.

gloss.en: a car

words.example: He works in the auto industry.

gloss.en: the freedom to govern itself independently

words.example: The region campaigned for greater autonomy.

gloss.en: the fact that something can be obtained

words.example: The availability of cheap flights has increased.

gloss.en: to wait for someone or something

words.example: He is in custody awaiting trial.

gloss.en: everything seen around an event or scene

words.example: The mountains provided a dramatic backdrop for our picnic.

gloss.en: help, support or financial support

words.example: The police gave the proposals their full backing.

gloss.en: extra help or support if needed

words.example: The police had backup from the army.

gloss.en: money paid so an accused person can stay free

words.example: Can anyone put up bail for you?

gloss.en: a system of voting, usually in secret

words.example: The chairperson is chosen by secret ballot.

gloss.en: a long cloth with a message shown in public

words.example: A huge banner over the street said 'Welcome home'.

gloss.en: not covered by any clothes

words.example: She likes to walk around in bare feet.

gloss.en: a large round container with flat ends

words.example: They stored the wine in a wooden barrel.

gloss.en: the lowest tone or part in music

words.example: He plays his stereo with the bass turned right up.

gloss.en: a piece of wood for hitting a ball in games

words.example: He bought a new baseball bat.

gloss.en: a place where a battle is fought

words.example: There were heavy casualties on the battlefield.

gloss.en: a part of the sea surrounded by curved land

words.example: They sailed across the bay at dawn.

gloss.en: a line of light or waves

words.example: Narrow beams of light came through the window.

gloss.en: an animal, especially a large or dangerous one

words.example: The jungle was full of wild beasts.
